# Summary

Briefly explain the problem and the approach taken.

## Changes
-
-

## Type of change

- [ ] feat (new feature)
- [ ] fix (bug fix)
- [ ] docs (documentation only)
- [ ] refactor (no functional change)
- [ ] test (add/update tests)
- [ ] ci/chore (build, tooling, deps)
- [ ] perf (performance improvements)

## Breaking changes

- [ ] No
- [ ] Yes — describe what breaks and migration steps:

## How to test

Provide steps or commands to validate the change locally and in CI.

## Linked issues

Link related issues using keywords, e.g. closes #123, fixes #456.

## Screenshots / Logs (optional)

If UI or relevant output changed, attach visuals or logs.

## Checklist

- [ ] Conventional Commits: commit messages (or PR title if squash-merge) follow the spec
- [ ] Tests added/updated and pass locally
- [ ] Documentation updated (README/CHANGELOG or in-repo docs)
- [ ] No secrets, keys, or sensitive data committed
- [ ] Considered edge cases and error handling
- [ ] I’ve self-reviewed the changes~~~~
